etymology.description: 品(品) (pǐn, "three mouths") + 木(木) (mù, "tree") = chirping of birds in a tree
etymology.interpretation: 喿(zào) depicts the sound of the chirping created by multiple birds in a tree, suggested by the multiple mouths and the tree components. It originally represented the meaning associated with the chirping of birds, which later evolved into 噪(噪) (zào, "noise") as a phonetic extension.
